data.title: PSQ Holdings notified by NYSE for non-compliance with continued listing standards
data.description:
• PSQ Holdings (NYSE: PSQH) on Tuesday said that it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange that it does not meet minimum requirements for total market capitalization, stockholders’ equity, and average closing price for its Class A common stock.
  • The company said the notice does not result in immediate delisting and that it intends to submit a business plan within 45 days to regain compliance with NYSE rules.
  • PSQ Holdings said it can restore compliance with the minimum price rule at any time within a six-month cure period if its stock closes at or above $1.00 on the last trading day of any month and averages at least $1.00 over the prior 30 trading days.
  • The company is considering available options to address the non-compliance.
  • PSQH -0.0% after hours to $0.7754.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sandisk dips on equity offering by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Sandisk (SNDK) has announced the launch of a secondary public offering of $3.09B common shares currently owned by Western Digital (WDC), the company’s former parent. 
  • The company is not selling any shares of common stock and will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the common shares in the offering or from the debt-for-equity exchange.

  • Before the offering closes, Western Digital (WDC) is expected to exchange its SanDisk (SNDK) shares for certain WDC debt held by affiliates of J.P. Morgan Securities LLC and BofA Securities.

  • Once the debt-for-equity exchange is completed, WDC is expected to transfer the SNDK shares—at the direction of the exchange counterparties—to J.P. Morgan Securities LLC and BofA Securities, who will act as selling stockholders in the offering.

  • If the exchange is finalized, the selling stockholders plan to sell the SNDK shares to the underwriters as part of the offering.

  • Sandisk has grown more than 1100% in the last 12 months.
  • SNDK -2.5% after hours to $583.82
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Devon Energy in charts: Marketing and midstream revenue slides 3% Y/Y
Berkshire Hathaway adds NY Times, boosts Chevron, pares BofA, Apple stakes among Q4 trades
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) (BRK.A), the investment giant built by Warren Buffett, added a new stake in the New York Times (NYT), increased its holdings in Chevron (CVX), and lightened its stakes in Bank of America (BAC) and Apple (AAPL), among other trades made in Q4, according to its 13F fiilng. 

The firm acquired 5.07M shares of the New York Times (NYT) with a value of $351.7M at the end of Q4 2025.

It boosted its stake in Chevron (CVX) to 130.2M shares ($19.8B) from 122.1M, Chubb (CB) to 34.3M shares ($10.7B) from 31.3M, and Domino's (DPZ) to 3.35M shares ($1.40B) from 2.98M.

Stake reductions were made in: Bank of America (BAC) to 517.3M ($23.5B) from 548.1M, Apple (APPL) to 227.9M shares ($62.0B) from 238.2M ($60.7B), and Amazon (AMZN) to 2.28M shares ($525.3M) from 10M shares, according to the filing.

While the company holds fewer shares of tech giant Apple than it did at the end of Q3, it's still Berkshire's biggest holding in its portfolio. Furthermore, the value of its Apple stake increased to $62.0B from ~$60.7B at the close of Q3.

data.title: Portland General Electric outlines $1.9B Washington acquisition and reaffirms long-term EPS growth outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Portland General Electric Company (POR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Maria Pope, President and CEO, announced "a definitive agreement to acquire the Washington electric utility business from PacifiCorp for $1.9 billion," stating that this move will expand PGE's portfolio by approximately 18% and add 140,000 customers in Washington State. Pope emphasized that the transaction "is forecast to be accretive in the first year, while diversifying and broadening our growth opportunities, underscoring long-term EPS and dividend growth of 5% to 7%."
  • Pope highlighted ongoing cost management, noting that "our teams exceeded targets for the -- and reduced PGE's overall cost structure by about $25 million."
  • Regarding customer growth, Pope reported "large customers, including high-tech manufacturers and especially data centers ramp their energy usage throughout the year, driving industrial growth of 14% compared to 2024."
  • Pope also stated, "we have signed build transfer agreements to construct a combined 125-megawatt solar and 125-megawatt battery storage facility at Biglow... [and] a combined 240-megawatt solar and 125-megawatt battery facility as part of the Wheatridge Expansion project."
  • Joseph Trpik, Senior VP of Finance & CFO, stated, "total load increased 3.8% overall and 4.7% weather-adjusted compared to 2024. Industrial load increased 14%, residential load decreased 1.8% year-over-year but increased 0.4% weather adjusted."
  • Trpik added, "total liquidity at the end of the year was $954 million. Our investment-grade credit ratings remain unchanged. Our outlook from Moody's has improved from negative to stable."

Outlook

  • Pope stated, "our 2026 earnings guidance [is] $3.33 to $3.53 per share. We are also reaffirming our long-term earnings and dividend growth guidance of 5% to 7%."
  • Trpik shared, "2026 weather-adjusted load growth guidance of 2.5% to 3.5%, long-term load growth guidance of 3% through 2030."
  • Management expects the Washington acquisition to be "accretive in the first year," with Pope noting, "this supports continued growth within that 5% to 7%."

Financial Results

  • Pope reported, "For the full year, we reported GAAP net income of $306 million or $2.77 per diluted share and non-GAAP net income of $336 million or $3.05 per share."
  • Weather impacts were noted: "This abnormal fourth quarter weather reduced earnings by $0.17."
  • Trpik stated, "After adjusting for business transformation and optimization expenses, we reached our 2025 non-GAAP EPS of $3.05 per diluted share."
  • Industrial customer growth remained strong, with year-over-year industrial load up 14%.

Q&A

  • Shahriar Pourreza, Wells Fargo: Asked about the accretion drivers of the Washington deal and sensitivities to regulatory timing and financing. Pope responded, "there are several key areas. The first is our permanent financing plans... We also are expecting our cost management plans to continue to be executed and integration of this new company will really help our cost structure."
  • Pourreza followed up on EPS growth rate enhancement, to which Pope affirmed, "we have a combination of factors that give us confidence to be squarely above the midpoint of our guidance range of 5% to 7%."
  • Julien Dumoulin-Smith, Jefferies: Inquired about earned ROEs and credit metrics. Trpik replied that the Washington business has "an imputed allowed ROE of 9.5%."
  • Christopher Ellinghaus, Siebert Williams Shank: Asked about the data center tariff's impact on residential customers. Pope replied, "the increase in data centers... directly benefits residential and small business customers. Initially, it's about a 2% reduction, and that should grow over time."
  • Anthony Crowdell, Mizuho: Sought details on the $600 million HoldCo financing. Trpik described it as "a balanced mix of debt, equity, potentially hybrid or other securities."
  • Andrew Levi, Hite Hedge: Queried about settlement timing for the holding company. Trpik indicated, "there are 2 scheduled settlement conferences... those settlement conferences do not yield a result, we -- there can be discussions that will continue up until once we get into the procedural part of a case and filing with an ultimate resolution required here at the end of June."
  • Steven Fleishman, Wolfe Research: Asked about approval standards in Oregon and Washington. Trpik stated, "in Oregon is a no harm standard... In Washington, it is a net benefit standard."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed keen interest and surprise at the Washington transaction, with Pourreza remarking, "Congrats on the deal. It's definitely interesting, really good transaction here, so unexpected."
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone throughout, emphasizing growth and accretion. Pope stated, "this supports continued growth within that 5% to 7%."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment was more focused on strategic implications and financial structuring, while management conveyed increased confidence due to the acquisition and customer growth drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured the announcement of a major $1.9 billion acquisition and entry into Washington State, a development absent in the previous quarter.
  • The company reaffirmed its long-term EPS and dividend growth guidance of 5% to 7%, consistent with prior guidance.
  • Analysts in this quarter probed deeper into accretion, regulatory standards, and the financing mix for the acquisition, reflecting heightened focus on capital structure and growth.
  • Management's tone was more assertive regarding growth drivers and integration plans compared to the previous quarter's focus on load growth and regulatory progress.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory approvals for the Washington acquisition are necessary, with Pope stating, "This acquisition will be subject to industry standard regulatory approvals, including from Washington, Oregon and other jurisdictions."
  • Unprecedented warm weather negatively impacted earnings by $0.17 in the fourth quarter.
  • Integration and cost structure improvements are critical for achieving projected accretion.
  • Regulatory lag and approval processes for both the acquisition and proposed holding company pose ongoing risks.

Final Takeaway

Portland General Electric Company is advancing a transformative $1.9 billion acquisition in Washington State, projecting immediate accretion and reinforcing its long-term EPS and dividend growth outlook of 5% to 7%. The company is leveraging strong industrial and data center demand, maintaining disciplined cost management, and focusing on constructive regulatory outcomes, all while navigating regulatory approval processes and integrating new assets to deliver shareholder value and broaden growth opportunities across the Pacific Northwest.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Westwood to liquidate LBRTY Global Equity ETF
data.description:
• Westwood Holdings Group (WHG) (NYSE: WHG) on Tuesday said that it plans to close and liquidate the Westwood LBRTY Global Equity ETF (NYSE Arca: BFRE) following an ongoing review of ETF offerings.
  • The company said the fund’s last day of trading on NYSE Arca will be February 27, 2026, which will also be the final day it will accept creation units from authorized participants.
  • Westwood said the fund is expected to cease operations, liquidate its assets and distribute proceeds to shareholders on March 6, 2026.
  • Shareholders who remain in the fund on the liquidation date will receive cash at the net asset value of their shares, including any capital gains and dividends.
  • The company said the liquidation may be treated as a taxable event.
  • WHG +0.12% after hours to $16.65.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: EQT Non-GAAP EPS of $0.90 beats by $0.14, revenue of $2.39B beats by $230M
data.description:
• EQT press release (EQT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.90 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $2.39B (+46.6% Y/Y) beats by $230M.

  • 2026 Outlook:

    • Production: 2026 production forecast of 2,275 – 2,375 Bcfe
    • Maintenance Capital Expenditures: 2026 maintenance capital spending guidance of $2,070 – $2,210 million
    • Growth Capital Expenditures: EQT has elected to invest its first $580 – $640 million of post-dividend free cash flow(1) in 2026 into high-return, infrastructure-focused growth projects
    • Free Cash Flow: Projecting ~$3.5 billion of free cash flow attributable to EQT(1) in 2026 at recent strip pricing, which includes the impact of ~$600 million in elected growth capital expenditures
    • Balance Sheet: Expect to exit 2026 with ~$4.7 billion of net debt(1) at recent strip pricing
